methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Recycleye is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including later-stage funded firms such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), and Dexterity ($1.6B), as well as earlier-stage players like Fairmat ($350M) and Collaborative Robotics ($500M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $48M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B, reflecting considerable dispersion in the cohort driven by outliers like 1X Technologies.

The two methods produce meaningfully different outputs, reflecting the sector's wide performance distribution. A triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $40M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $561M. The stage-progression method anchors the downside; peer median establishes the upside. Key uncertainties include Recycleye's relative competitive positioning within this heterogeneous peer set and whether its technology and market trajectory more closely track early-stage or later-stage peer valuations. Resolution of these factors will be critical to narrowing the $1.2B range.
